project: Portfolio v2 — Blog, Assets, i18n e Polish
description: Evolução do portfólio: corrigir bugs da v1, internacionalizar em PT-BR/EN/ES com next-intl, criar seção 'Meus Assets' (code snippets curados) e blog com MDX.

userStories.title: Fix — Fonte Varela Round não carrega
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want the site to display Varela Round font correctly instead of Times New Roman.
acceptanceCriteria: Varela Round loads correctly via next/font/google
CSS variable --font-sans resolves to Varela Round across the entire application
Fallback to generic sans-serif if font fails to load
Geist Mono continues working for mono font
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Font was switched from next/font/local to next/font/google but still shows Times New Roman. Investigate the CSS variable chain: layout.tsx sets --font-varela-round on html, globals.css @theme inline sets --font-sans: var(--font-varela-round). Check if @theme inline can reference runtime CSS vars.

userStories.title: Fix — Image fill sem position no project-card
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to fix the next/image warning about fill prop parent without relative position.
acceptanceCriteria: motion.div parent of Image in project-card.tsx has className='relative'
Warning 'parent element with invalid position' no longer appears in console
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: The relative class was already added but verify the fix is complete and no other images have the same issue.

userStories.title: Fix — Revisão geral de responsividade
userStories.description: As a mobile visitor, I want all pages to work perfectly on small screens.
acceptanceCriteria: Home: hero, marquee, featured projects, about preview, CTA — all visually correct at 375px, 768px and 1440px
Projects: listing and filter work on mobile
Project detail: hero, gallery, results, navigation — all responsive
About: hero, bio, skills, timeline — all responsive
Navbar mobile drawer works without bugs
No horizontal overflow on any page
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use dev-browser to check each page at 375px and 768px widths.

userStories.title: Setup i18n — Install next-intl and restructure routing
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to configure next-intl for i18n with locale-based routing under /pt, /en, /es.
acceptanceCriteria: next-intl installed
Middleware created for locale detection based on Accept-Language header
Default locale is 'pt', supported locales: pt, en, es
Redirect from / to detected locale (default /pt)
src/app/[locale]/layout.tsx created wrapping existing layout content
All existing pages moved under src/app/[locale]/ (home, projects, projects/[slug], about, not-found)
Existing pages work at /<locale>/... routes (e.g. /pt/projects, /en/about)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a structural change. Move pages from src/app/ to src/app/[locale]/. Keep root layout.tsx minimal (html, body). The [locale] layout handles ThemeProvider, Navbar, Footer. Use next-intl's createNavigation, routing config, and middleware. Consult next-intl docs and Next.js 16 docs for compatibility. params is a Promise in Next.js 16.

userStories.title: i18n — Translation files for all 3 languages
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to create translation files for PT-BR, EN and ES with all static site content.
acceptanceCriteria: Translation files created: messages/pt.json, messages/en.json, messages/es.json
Sections translated: navbar, footer, hero, tech-stack, featured-projects, about-preview, CTA, projects page, project detail, about page, 404
EN and ES translations are natural (not literal Google Translate)
useTranslations() hook available for components via next-intl
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Focus on quality translations. Project data (titles, descriptions) stays in PT-BR — only UI labels are translated.

userStories.title: i18n — Update existing components to use translations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to update all existing components to use translations instead of hardcoded strings.
acceptanceCriteria: Navbar: links translated by locale
Hero: heading, subtitle and CTA translated
Footer: texts translated
All home sections use translations
Projects, about and 404 pages use translations
Project data (title, description) stays in PT-BR (real content, not translated)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Replace all hardcoded Portuguese strings with useTranslations() calls. Server components use getTranslations() from next-intl/server.

userStories.title: i18n — Language selector in navbar
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to switch the site language via a selector in the navbar.
acceptanceCriteria: Language selector in navbar (next to theme toggle) using shadcn dropdown-menu
Options: Português, English, Español with text labels
Switching redirects to same page in new locale (e.g. /pt/projects → /en/projects)
Selected locale persists via cookie
Metadata (og:locale, hreflang) updated per locale
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use next-intl's usePathname and useRouter for locale-aware navigation. Use Globe icon from lucide-react for the trigger.

userStories.title: Syntax highlighting setup with rehype-pretty-code
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to configure syntax highlighting for code blocks in blog and assets.
acceptanceCriteria: rehype-pretty-code and shiki installed and configured in next.config.ts via createMDX
Syntax highlighting theme works with both dark and light mode
Optional line numbers in code blocks
Line highlighting (highlight specific lines)
File title in code blocks (e.g. ```tsx title='components/button.tsx'```)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Configure in next.config.ts createMDX options. Try github-dark-default for dark mode and github-light-default for light mode. This must be done before blog and asset pages that need code rendering.

userStories.title: Blog — MDX custom components (Callout, CodeBlock, Image)
userStories.description: As an author, I want to use React components inside MDX posts to enrich content.
acceptanceCriteria: mdx-components.tsx updated with custom components
Callout component with variants: info, warning, error
CodeBlock component with copy button using Clipboard API
Image component wrapping next/image with optimization
Styles consistent with design system (colors, spacing, typography)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: These components are used inside MDX content for both blog posts and potentially asset explanations.

userStories.title: Meus Assets — Data layer and types
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to define the data structure for code assets.
acceptanceCriteria: Type CodeAsset defined in src/data/assets.ts

Type includes: slug, title, description, locale ('pt' | 'en' | 'es'), language (ts, js, python, go, bash, css, etc.), code (string), explanation (string), tags (string[]), category (utils, hooks, patterns, algorithms, devops), createdAt (string), updatedAt (string optional)
8-10 mock assets with real code and useful explanations (e.g. debounce, custom fetch hook, retry pattern, docker compose snippet, useful regex) — majority locale 'pt', 2-3 locale 'en'
Utility functions: getAssetBySlug, getAssetsByCategory, getAssetsByTag, getAllAssetCategories, getAllAssetTags, getAssetsByLocale
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Assets are TypeScript data, not MDX files. The code field is a raw string, explanation is markdown. Include a getAssetsByLocale(locale) utility.

userStories.title: Meus Assets — Listing page
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to browse the dev's curated code assets, filtering by category or language.
acceptanceCriteria: Route /<locale>/assets created
Heading with TextReveal animation
Filter bar: by category (toggle-group) and/or text search
Listing filters assets by current route locale (e.g. /pt/assets shows only locale 'pt')
Grid of cards with: title, language (colored badge), short description, tags, code preview (3-4 truncated lines with syntax highlighting)
Cards clickable → /<locale>/assets/[slug]
Stagger animation on cards
SEO metadata
Responsive layout
i18n translations for static labels
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Do NOT add the Assets link to navbar here — that is done in US-017. Focus on the page itself.

userStories.title: Meus Assets — Detail page
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see a specific asset with full code, syntax highlighting and detailed explanation.
acceptanceCriteria: Route /<locale>/assets/[slug] created
generateStaticParams for all asset slugs
Asset title with language badge
Full code block with syntax highlighting
Copy code button using shadcn button + Clipboard API with visual feedback
Explanation rendered as markdown with prose styling
Tags as clickable badges (link to listing filtered by tag)
Prev/next navigation between assets
Dynamic metadata via generateMetadata
i18n translations for static labels
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Use rehype-pretty-code for the code block. params is a Promise in Next.js 16 — use await params.

userStories.title: Blog — Data layer with MDX
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to set up the blog data layer with MDX for rich posts.
acceptanceCriteria: Type BlogPost defined in src/data/blog-posts.ts

Type includes: slug, title, description, locale ('pt' | 'en' | 'es'), date (string), updatedAt (string optional), tags (string[]), category (string), readingTime (string), coverImage (string optional), published (boolean)
Posts as MDX files in content/blog/ with frontmatter exporting metadata
Function to calculate reading time based on content
Utility functions: getAllPosts (sorted by date), getPostBySlug, getPostsByTag, getPostsByCategory, getPostsByLocale
2-3 placeholder posts with relevant technical content — majority locale 'pt', 1 locale 'en'
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Blog posts are MDX files, unlike assets which are TypeScript data. Each MDX file has frontmatter with locale field. Include getPostsByLocale(locale) utility.

userStories.title: Blog — Listing page
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to browse blog posts with filters and search.
acceptanceCriteria: Route /<locale>/blog created
Heading with TextReveal animation
Post cards with: title, date formatted by locale, reading time, description, tags, cover image (if any)
Filter by category and/or text search
Listing filters posts by current route locale (e.g. /en/blog shows only locale 'en')
Posts sorted by date (most recent first)
Posts with published: false do not appear in listing
Stagger animation on cards
SEO metadata
Responsive layout
i18n translations for static labels
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Do NOT add the Blog link to navbar here — that is done in US-017. Focus on the page itself.

userStories.title: Blog — Post page
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to read a full blog post with nice typography and syntax highlighting.
acceptanceCriteria: Route /<locale>/blog/[slug] created
generateStaticParams for all post slugs
Header: title, date, reading time, tags, cover image
MDX content rendered with @tailwindcss/typography prose classes
Syntax highlighting in code blocks via rehype-pretty-code
Headings with clickable anchor links
Prev/next navigation between posts
Dynamic metadata (title, description, og:image) via generateMetadata
Loading skeleton with shadcn skeleton
i18n translations for static labels
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: params is a Promise in Next.js 16 — use await params.

userStories.title: Navbar updated with new links and language selector
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want to see Assets and Blog links in the navbar with i18n support and language selector.
acceptanceCriteria: Navbar displays links: Home, Projects, Assets, Blog, About
Links translated by locale
Active state works for all routes including new ones
Mobile drawer updated with all links
Language selector integrated (from US-007)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: This story consolidates all navbar changes. Assets link goes between Projects and Blog. Blog between Assets and About.

userStories.title: Sitemap and SEO updated for new pages and locales
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to update sitemap and metadata to include new pages and all locales.
acceptanceCriteria: sitemap.ts includes all routes for assets and blog
sitemap.ts generates URLs for all 3 locales
robots.ts updated if necessary
hreflang tags on pages for alternate locales
Each page's metadata includes correct locale
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Polish — Refined animations and transitions
userStories.description: As a visitor, I want smoother and more polished animations across all pages.
acceptanceCriteria: Review timing of all animations (scroll reveal, stagger, counters)
Page transitions between pages (Motion AnimatePresence or View Transitions API)
Consistent hover effects on all cards (projects, assets, blog)
Loading states with skeleton on all dynamic pages
prefers-reduced-motion respected in all new components
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
